milestones.title: Further reduce Haiku 4.5 synthesis time in async Lambda (currently 23.4s avg)
milestones.description: Two-phase synthesis shipped (PR #156, Mar 31). Phase 1 streams Key Points to DynamoDB as partial result (~8s). Client polls for Key Points while Phase 2 generates full answer (~18s). Down from 23.4s full wait. Further optimization: compact prompt active (SYNTHESIS_PROMPT_COMPACT=1). Lambda handler + polling API updated.

milestones.title: Improve PubMed query construction (0 results on valid topics)
milestones.description: PubMed query construction improved in src/lib/pubmed-search.ts. Boolean query generation, MeSH term expansion, and fallback to title-abstract search.
milestones.quickPrompt: In the Evidence Engine repo, fix PubMed query construction in src/lib/pubmed-search.ts. Some valid clinical queries return 0 results due to overly strict Boolean logic. Simplify to title-abstract search with MeSH fallback.
milestones.fullPrompt:
## Task: Fix PubMed Query Construction

**Repo:** sevaro-evidence-engine
**Key file:** src/lib/pubmed-search.ts

## Problem
Some valid clinical queries return 0 PubMed results. Root cause: Boolean query too strict (AND chaining all terms).

## Fix
1. Default to title-abstract [tiab] search instead of all-fields
2. Add MeSH term expansion as fallback
3. Retry with simplified query if 0 results

## Acceptance criteria
- 'levetiracetam focal epilepsy' returns > 0 results
- 'aspirin stroke prevention' returns > 0 results
- No regression on queries that currently work

milestones.title: Deploy document-processor + kb-sync Lambdas for automated KB updates
milestones.description: KB sync Lambdas deployed. EventBridge schedule triggers weekly. S3 event trigger on active/external/ for sidecar auto-generation.
milestones.quickPrompt: In the Evidence Engine repo, automate KB updates with a kb-sync Lambda that triggers Bedrock KB re-ingestion after new documents are added to S3. Use EventBridge schedule + S3 event trigger. Lambda IAM role needs bedrock:StartIngestionJob.
milestones.fullPrompt:
## Task: Automated KB Update Pipeline

**Repo:** sevaro-evidence-engine
**New files:** lambda/kb-sync/, lambda/document-processor/

## Context
KB updates currently require manual console clicks. Need automation.

## Build
1. Lambda triggered by S3 events on active/external/ prefix
2. Calls bedrock:StartIngestionJob on KB T4W8S8RNMN
3. EventBridge schedule for weekly full sync
4. CloudWatch alarm on failed ingestions

milestones.title: SSO Phase 4 — Passkeys/WebAuthn as TOTP alternative
milestones.description: Add WebAuthn/Passkeys as an alternative to TOTP MFA. User-requested, deferred until SSO is fully stable. Current MFA: required TOTP via Cognito. Target: allow passkey registration + use in Cognito User Pool.
milestones.quickPrompt: In the sevaro-evidence-engine repo, add Passkeys/WebAuthn as a TOTP alternative. Cognito User Pool: us-east-2_9y6XyJnXC. Context (May 14, 2026): Production-readiness audit fully closed — all 44 findings merged (PRs #529-560); security posture, observability, and test coverage are complete. CI gate active (typecheck + node tests on every PR). Extension self-service Forgot Password + MFA setup shipped (PR #570). Multi-patient rounding chart-prep Phases 1-3d complete (v2.1.33). Clinical safety hardening Phases 1.1-1.5 merged (DDI tool, coverage gate, inline validation). This milestone (passkeys) is additive auth UX, not a security gap. Check passkey support: aws cognito-idp describe-user-pool --user-pool-id us-east-2_9y6XyJnXC --profile sevaro-sandbox. Implement passkey registration in src/app/settings/ and passkey sign-in in src/lib/cognito.ts. Keep existing TOTP path — passkeys are additive.
milestones.fullPrompt:
## Task: SSO Phase 4 - Passkeys/WebAuthn

**Repo:** sevaro-evidence-engine
**Key files:** src/app/auth/, src/lib/cognito.ts

## Context
Current auth: Cognito + required TOTP MFA. Users find TOTP friction high. Request: add passkey as alternative.

## Approach
1. Check Cognito passkey support on current pool settings
2. Enable WebAuthn in the Cognito User Pool if supported
3. Add passkey registration flow to the auth UI (after initial TOTP setup)
4. Add passkey sign-in as an alternative on the login page
5. Keep TOTP as fallback

## Acceptance Criteria
- User can register a passkey after first login
- User can authenticate with passkey on subsequent visits
- TOTP still works as fallback
- No regression on existing auth flow

## Constraints
- Never bypass TOTP requirement on first login
- All Cognito changes via CDK or AWS CLI with --profile sevaro-sandbox
- Test with ephemeral test user before production changes
manualSteps: Check Cognito passkey support: aws cognito-idp describe-user-pool --user-pool-id us-east-2_9y6XyJnXC --profile sevaro-sandbox
Enable WebAuthn MFA option in Cognito User Pool settings
Build passkey registration component in src/app/settings/
Add passkey auth flow to src/lib/cognito.ts
Add Sign in with passkey button to login page
Test with ephemeral test user (sevaro-ops scripts)

milestones.title: Live voice streaming during encounters
milestones.description: Currently transcription only works post-recording. Add real-time voice streaming during clinical encounters so clinicians see words appearing as they speak. This requires WebSocket integration with a streaming transcription service.
prerequisites: AWS Transcribe or Deepgram API access
milestones.quickPrompt: In the OPSAmple repo, add real-time voice streaming to the main encounter transcription flow. Context (May 14, 2026): The /consult Historian already uses Realtime API (WebRTC) for live voice; this milestone is for the general encounter transcription workflow (non-Historian). Recording durability sprint (PRs #508-512), multi-patient rounding v2.1 (PR #523), extension tab-capture hardening (PRs #513-518), and triage async+polling (PRs #112-113) all shipped. sevaro-evidence-engine production-readiness audit is fully closed — all 44 findings merged (PRs #529-560); clinical safety hardening Phases 1.1-1.5 merged (DDI tool, coverage gate, inline validation); rounding chart-prep Phases 1-3d complete (v2.1.33). No active commits on live-voice-streaming since May 5 — this is the next priority. Use AWS Transcribe Medical (HIPAA-eligible, BAA-eligible) — same chunked WebM → chunk-concat Lambda → Transcribe Medical pattern. Reference: sevaro-evidence-engine chrome-extension/src/services/ and chrome-extension/src/offscreen/ for the full streaming pipeline. Target: words appear within 500ms of being spoken in the encounter view.
milestones.fullPrompt:
## Task: Real-Time Voice Streaming

**Repo:** OPSAmplehtml
**Key area:** Transcription components

## Context
OPSAmple has post-recording transcription but no live streaming. Clinicians want to see words appear in real-time during encounters.

## Approach
1. Add WebSocket connection to streaming transcription service
2. Display interim results in real-time, replace with finals
3. Handle microphone permissions and audio capture in browser
4. Consider AWS Transcribe Medical for HIPAA compliance

## Acceptance criteria
- Words appear within 500ms of being spoken
- Final transcription matches post-recording quality
- Works on Chrome and Safari
- Graceful fallback if streaming unavailable

milestones.title: Fill remaining 18 diagnosis treatment plans (148/166 covered)
milestones.description: The diagnosis plan database covers 148 of 166 target diagnoses. The remaining 18 need clinical content drafted and loaded into the database. Each plan includes treatment options, monitoring parameters, and clinical decision points.
milestones.quickPrompt: In the OPSAmple repo, complete the remaining diagnosis treatment plans. Current state (May 14, 2026): 98 plans in RDS neuro_plans table, 148 of 166 target diagnoses covered — 18 diagnoses still lack plans. No active work on this since May 5. sevaro-evidence-engine audit fully closed (all 44 findings, PRs #529-560). Query `SELECT diagnosis_name FROM diagnoses WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT diagnosis_id FROM neuro_plans)` (adjust to actual schema) to identify which 18 are missing. Draft clinical content for each (treatment options, monitoring parameters, decision points) following the format of existing plans. Use Evidence Engine for clinical guidance on rare diagnoses. Load into RDS via the existing sync pipeline (`npm run sync-plans`). Verify all 166 diagnoses surface plans in the app UI.
milestones.fullPrompt:
## Task: Complete Diagnosis Plan Coverage

**Repo:** OPSAmplehtml

## Context
148 of 166 target diagnoses have treatment plans. Need to identify and create the remaining 18.

## Steps
1. Query RDS to find which 18 diagnoses are missing
2. Draft clinical content for each (treatment options, monitoring, decision points)
3. Follow the same format as existing plans
4. Load into database

## Acceptance criteria
- All 166 diagnoses have treatment plans
- Content follows existing plan format
- Plans are accessible in the app
manualSteps: Draft treatment plan content for each
Review with clinical accuracy in mind

milestones.title: Speaker diarization UI
milestones.description: Add speaker identification to transcription output so clinicians can see who said what during multi-person encounters. Requires diarization from the transcription service and UI to display labeled speaker turns.
milestones.quickPrompt: In the OPSAmple repo, add speaker diarization UI to the transcription view. Show labeled speaker turns (Doctor, Patient, Nurse) in the transcript. Context (May 14, 2026): triage pipeline uses async+polling pattern (PR #112, migration 046 dropped NOT NULL constraints); /consult Historian uses WebRTC Realtime API; transcription stack is AWS Transcribe Medical (HIPAA). This milestone depends on live-voice-streaming (higher priority) — diarization builds on top of the streaming transcription infrastructure. When implementing, enable speaker diarization in the Transcribe Medical streaming API call and render speaker-labeled turns in the transcript UI.
milestones.fullPrompt:
## Task: Speaker Diarization UI

**Repo:** OPSAmplehtml

## Context (May 9, 2026)
Multi-person encounters need speaker identification. The transcription service supports diarization but the UI doesn't display it.

- Triage pipeline: async+polling pattern (PR #112, migration 046)
- /consult Historian: WebRTC Realtime API already in use
- Transcription stack: AWS Transcribe Medical (HIPAA-eligible)
- live-voice-streaming milestone is higher priority — implement first

## Build
1. Enable speaker diarization in Transcribe Medical streaming API calls
2. Parse speaker labels from transcription output
3. Display speaker turns with labels and color coding
4. Allow manual speaker label editing (rename Speaker 1 to 'Dr. Smith')

## Acceptance criteria
- Speaker turns are visually distinct
- Labels are editable
- Works with 2-6 speakers
manualSteps: Enable diarization parameter in API calls

milestones.fullPrompt:
## Task: Complete Supabase Migration

**Repo:** OPSAmplehtml

## Context
Most OPSAmple data is in RDS but some tables/auth still use Supabase. Need to fully migrate.

## Steps
1. Audit: grep -r 'supabase' src/ to find remaining usage
2. For each Supabase query: create equivalent RDS migration + node-postgres query
3. For auth: replace Supabase auth with Cognito JWT verification
4. Remove Supabase SDK dependency

## Acceptance criteria
- No Supabase imports remain in src/
- All data is in RDS
- Auth uses Cognito JWT throughout
